table_of_contents.title:
LEAST-TO-MOST PROMPTING ENABLES COMPLEX
REASONING IN LARGE LANGUAGE MODELS

Least-to-most prompting (Reducing) Least-to-most prompting (Solving)
Q: The gender distribution of the pop-
ulation was 50.2% male and 49.8% fe-
male. Of the adult population, 29 peo-
ple or 14.6% of the population are be-
tween 20 and 29 years old. 28 people
or 14.1% are 30 to 39, 36 people or
18.2% are 40 to 49, and 31 people or
15.7% are 50 to 59. How many per-
cent of people are not 40 to 49?
A: To answer the question "How many
percent of people are not 40 to 49?',
we need to know "How many percent
of people are 40 to 49?"
The gender distribution of the population was 50.2%
male and 49.8% female. Of the adult population, 29
people or 14.6% of the population are between 20 and
29 years old. 28 people or 14.1% are 30 to 39, 36
people or 18.2% are 40 to 49, and 31 people or 15.7%
are 50 to 59.
Q: How many percent of people are 40 to 49?
A: "36 people or 18.2% are 40 to 49". So the answer
is 18.2%.
Q: How many percent of people are not 40 to 49?
A: We know that 18.2% are 40 to 49. So 100% - 18.2%
= 81.8% are not 40 to 49. So the answer is 81.8%.
A.2 DATA GENERATION AND ADDITIONAL RESULTS
